title: Aeonium × riosjordanae
taxonomy: Aeonium × riosjordanae is a hybrid succulent plant, which belongs to the family Crassulaceae. It is a cross between the species Aeonium canariense and Aeonium smithii. As a hybrid, Aeonium × riosjordanae is not assigned a botanical species name, but rather a nothospecific name. The plant is known for its striking rosettes and vibrant colors. It is typically grown indoors or in protected outdoor environments due to its sensitivity to frost and extreme temperatures.

intro: Aeonium × riosjordanae, commonly known as Aeonium Río Jordanae or Río Jordanae Aeonium, is a rare and hybrid succulent plant species native to the Canary Islands. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of conditions such as arthritis and oxidative stress. Research has identified the plant's bioactive constituents to include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Aeonium Río Jordanae is used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, although further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
